method.name: attach_database
method.description: Attaches an external SQLite database located at [param path] to the current connection using the alias [param alias]. This allows queries to reference multiple databases within the same connection.

method.name: backup_async
method.description: Creates an asynchronous backup of the current database to [param path]. Returns a [SQLiteBackup] object that can be used to monitor progress.

method.name: backup_to
method.description: Creates a full backup of the database and writes it to [param path]. Returns [code]true[/code] if the operation succeeds.

method.name: begin_transaction
method.description: Begins a new database transaction. All subsequent changes will be part of this transaction until committed or rolled back.

method.name: close
method.description: Closes the database connection and releases all associated resources.

method.name: commit_transaction
method.description: Commits the current transaction, permanently applying all changes made during the transaction.

method.name: create_aggregate
method.description: Creates a custom aggregate SQL function named [param name] that processes [param argc] arguments using [param step_callable] for iteration and [param final_callable] for final result computation.

method.name: create_collation
method.description: Creates a custom collation function named [param name] using [param callable] to define string comparison rules.

method.name: create_function
method.description: Creates a custom SQL function named [param name] that takes [param argc] arguments and executes [param callable].

method.name: create_index
method.description: Creates an index named [param index_name] on [param table_name] using the specified [param columns]. If [param unique] is true, enforces uniqueness.

method.name: create_query
method.description: Creates a prepared SQL query from [param query] with optional [param arguments].

method.name: create_savepoint
method.description: Creates a savepoint named [param name] inside the current transaction.

method.name: create_table
method.description: Creates a table named [param table_name] using the provided [param columns] schema definition.

method.name: delete_rows
method.description: Deletes rows from [param table_name] that match the SQL [code]WHERE[/code] clause in [param condition]. If empty, all rows may be affected.

method.name: deserialize_object
method.description: Deserializes data stored in [param table_name] under [param key] into [param object].

method.name: detach_database
method.description: Detaches a previously attached database identified by [param alias].

method.name: drop_index
method.description: Drops the index named [param index_name] from the database.

method.name: drop_table
method.description: Drops the table named [param table_name] from the database.

method.name: enable_load_extension
method.description: Enables or disables SQLite extension loading.

method.name: execute_query
method.description: Executes the SQL [param query] with optional [param arguments] and returns the result set.

method.name: execute_script
method.description: Executes an SQL script located at [param path].

method.name: export_to_json
method.description: Exports the contents of [param table_name] as a JSON string.

method.name: get_changes
method.description: Returns the number of rows affected by the most recent operation.

method.name: get_columns
method.description: Returns the column schema information for [param table_name].

method.name: get_database_filename
method.description: Returns the filename of the database identified by [param db_name].

method.name: get_db_config
method.description: Returns the database configuration value identified by [param op].

method.name: get_db_status
method.description: Returns the database status value for [param op]. If [param reset] is true, the counter is reset.

method.name: get_global_status
method.description: Returns a global SQLite status counter for [param op].

method.name: get_last_error_code
method.description: Returns the last error code produced by the database.

method.name: get_last_error_message
method.description: Returns the last error message produced by the database.

method.name: get_last_insert_rowid
method.description: Returns the row ID of the last inserted record.

method.name: get_limit
method.description: Returns the SQLite limit value identified by [param id].

method.name: get_tables
method.description: Returns a dictionary containing all tables in the database and their metadata.

method.name: get_total_changes
method.description: Returns the total number of changes made since the connection was opened.

method.name: import_from_json
method.description: Imports data into [param table_name] from a JSON string.

method.name: insert_row
method.description: Inserts a single row into [param table_name] using [param value] as column data.

method.name: insert_rows
method.description: Inserts multiple rows into [param table_name].

method.name: instantiate_object
method.description: Creates an object instance from database data.

method.name: interrupt
method.description: Interrupts any running query.

method.name: is_autocommit
method.description: Returns true if the database is in autocommit mode.

method.name: is_readonly
method.description: Returns true if the database [param db_name] is read-only.

method.name: load_from
method.description: Loads a database from [param path].

method.name: open_blob
method.description: Opens a BLOB for direct access.

method.name: release_memory
method.description: Releases up to [param bytes] of SQLite internal memory.

method.name: release_savepoint
method.description: Releases the savepoint [param name].

method.name: restore_async
method.description: Restores a database asynchronously from [param path].

method.name: restore_from
method.description: Restores the database from [param path].

method.name: rollback_to_savepoint
method.description: Rolls back to savepoint [param name].

method.name: rollback_transaction
method.description: Rolls back the current transaction.

method.name: select_rows
method.description: Selects rows from [param table_name] matching [param condition].

method.name: serialize_object
method.description: Serializes [param object] into the database.

method.name: set_authorizer
method.description: Sets an authorization callback.

method.name: set_busy_timeout
method.description: Sets the busy timeout to [param ms] milliseconds.

method.name: set_db_config
method.description: Sets database configuration option [param op].

method.name: set_foreign_keys_enabled
method.description: Enables or disables foreign keys.

method.name: set_limit
method.description: Sets a SQLite limit.

method.name: set_progress_handler
method.description: Sets a progress callback.

method.name: set_soft_heap_limit
method.description: Sets SQLite soft heap limit.

method.name: set_trace
method.description: Enables or disables SQL tracing.

method.name: update_rows
method.description: Updates rows in [param table_name] matching [param condition].

method.name: vacuum
method.description: Rebuilds the database to reclaim space.

method.name: wal_checkpoint
method.description: Performs a WAL checkpoint on [param db_name].

members.member: The underlying [SQLiteDatabase] resource managed by this node. It represents the actual SQLite database instance used for executing queries, managing tables, and performing transactions.

signal.name: query_progress
signal.description: Emitted periodically while a long-running SQL query is executing. This can be used to monitor progress or implement cancellation logic during heavy database operations.

signal.name: row_updated
signal.description: Emitted whenever a row is inserted, updated, or deleted. The type of operation is given by [param update_operation], while [param db_name] and [param table_name] identify the affected database and table. The modified row is identified by [param rowid].

signal.name: transaction_committed
signal.description: Emitted when a database transaction is successfully committed and all changes are permanently applied.

signal.name: transaction_rolled_back
signal.description: Emitted when a database transaction is rolled back and all uncommitted changes are discarded.

signal.name: wal_updated
signal.description: Emitted when the Write-Ahead Logging (WAL) file is updated. The [param db_name] specifies the database that was modified, and [param pages_in_wal] indicates how many pages are currently stored in the WAL.
